articles.title: For Most of the World, Open-Source AI Is the Only Way Forward
articles.summary: Open-source AI is emerging as the only viable path forward for most countries and companies due to its ability to mediate digital interactions with information more generally, far beyond today's search engines. Yann LeCun, one of the "Godfathers of AI," has framed AI as an infrastructure-level platform that will soon enable global communication with information, but warned that if dominated by proprietary systems from big tech companies on the West Coast and China, it could lead to cultural diversity, linguistic diversity, democracy, and human rights issues. LeCun argues that most countries cannot afford to build their own large language models (LLMs) but can contribute to an open-source platform, which he believes could surpass proprietary systems in terms of its ability to drive progress transparently, equitably, and humanely.

articles.title: PR spam today looks like email spam in the early 2000s
articles.summary: PR spam today looks like email spam in the early 2000s when it comes to open source contribution, with large numbers of low-effort slop submissions flooding repositories and causing a spike in merge rates for many projects. This phenomenon is reminiscent of the ILOVEYOU worm that infected millions of computers in 24 hours due to the lack of effective filtering measures at the time. As a result, contributors are being filtered by their reputation, with certain groups being more likely to be blocked or flagged than others.
